title: Introduction to slitherlinkshiny
text: Slitherlink logic puzzle played rectangular grid dots. goal draw segments adjacent dots form single closed loop — branches, crossings, loose ends. Numbers inside cells indicate exactly many cell’s four edges belong loop. Cells without number unconstrained. unique solution 3×3 puzzle outer rectangle.

title: Introduction to slitherlinkshiny
text: package stores puzzle slitherlink_grid object three components: segment one three integer values:

title: Introduction to slitherlinkshiny
text: Use init_grid() build grid clue matrix. NA marks cells constraint.
code: library(slitherlinkshiny)  clues <- matrix(   c(2, 1, 2,     1, 0, 1,     2, 1, 2),   nrow = 3, byrow = TRUE ) g <- init_grid(clues) print(g) #> Slitherlink Grid (3x3) #>  #> +   +   +   + #>   2    1    2    #> +   +   +   + #>   1    0    1    #> +   +   +   + #>   2    1    2    #> +   +   +   +

title: Introduction to slitherlinkshiny
text: package ships three ready--play puzzles. Load one get_puzzle():
code: list_puzzles() #>                  name difficulty size verified #> easy_3x3     easy_3x3       easy  3x3     TRUE #> medium_4x4 medium_4x4     medium  4x4     TRUE #> hard_5x5     hard_5x5       hard  5x5     TRUE g <- get_puzzle("medium_4x4") print(g) #> Slitherlink Grid (4x4) #>  #> +   +   +   +   + #>   2    2    2    1    #> +   +   +   +   + #>   1    1    2    3    #> +   +   +   +   + #>   2    2    1    1    #> +   +   +   +   + #>   3    1    0    0    #> +   +   +   +   +

title: Introduction to slitherlinkshiny
text: toggle_segment() cycles segment three states: 0 → 1 → -1 → 0. type = "h" targets horizontal segment row , column j (row index counts top; ranges 1 n+1). type = "v" targets vertical segment (1 n, j 1 m+1).
code: g <- get_puzzle("easy_3x3")  # Draw the top edge of cell (1,1): horizontal segment h[1,1] g <- toggle_segment(g, type = "h", i = 1, j = 1) g$seg_h[1, 1]   # now 1 (drawn) #> [1] 1  # Draw it again to cross it g <- toggle_segment(g, type = "h", i = 1, j = 1) g$seg_h[1, 1]   # now -1 (crossed) #> [1] -1  # Draw it once more to reset g <- toggle_segment(g, type = "h", i = 1, j = 1) g$seg_h[1, 1]   # back to 0 (empty) #> [1] 0

title: Introduction to slitherlinkshiny
text: Three functions check current state grid.

title: Introduction to slitherlinkshiny
text: Verifies clue constraint violated. strict = FALSE (default -progress play): drawn edges must exceed clue. strict = TRUE: drawn edges must exactly equal clue.
code: g <- get_puzzle("easy_3x3") check_clues(g, strict = FALSE)   # TRUE  — nothing drawn yet #> [1] TRUE check_clues(g, strict = TRUE)    # FALSE — no clue is exactly matched #> [1] FALSE

title: Introduction to slitherlinkshiny
text: Returns TRUE drawn segments form single valid closed loop (every active node degree exactly 2, active nodes connected).
code: check_loop(g)   # FALSE — no segments drawn #> [1] FALSE

title: Introduction to slitherlinkshiny
text: puzzle solved check_clues(strict = TRUE) check_loop() return TRUE.

title: Introduction to slitherlinkshiny
text: solve_grid() uses constraint propagation followed backtracking find unique solution. solver works three built-puzzles: puzzle valid solution, solve_grid() returns NULL:
code: g   <- get_puzzle("easy_3x3") sol <- solve_grid(g) print(sol) #> Slitherlink Grid (3x3) #>  #> +---+---+---+ #> | 2  x 1  x 2  | #> + x + x + x + #> | 1  x 0  x 1  | #> + x + x + x + #> | 2  x 1  x 2  | #> +---+---+---+ is_solved(sol) #> [1] TRUE for (nm in list_puzzles()$name) {   sol <- solve_grid(get_puzzle(nm))   cat(nm, "→ solved:", is_solved(sol), "\n") } #> easy_3x3 → solved: TRUE  #> medium_4x4 → solved: TRUE  #> hard_5x5 → solved: TRUE impossible <- init_grid(matrix(2L, 1, 1))  # clue 2 in a 1x1 grid: impossible is.null(solve_grid(impossible)) #> [1] TRUE

title: Introduction to slitherlinkshiny
text: package includes full interactive web application. Launch R console: Features: Select puzzle — choose built-library. New Game / Reset — load restart selected puzzle. Click segments — click near edge cycle state (empty → drawn → crossed → empty). Solve — fill complete solution instantly. Status indicator — shows progress, Constraint violated, Puzzle solved! real time.

title: slitherlinkshiny — Interactive Slitherlink Puzzle
text: R package playing solving Slitherlink logic puzzles, full interactive Shiny application.

title: slitherlinkshiny — Interactive Slitherlink Puzzle
text: Slitherlink logic puzzle played rectangular grid dots. goal draw segments adjacent dots form single closed loop — branches, crossings, dead ends. Numbers inside cells indicate exactly many cell’s four edges belong loop. unique solution 3×3 puzzle outer rectangle.

title: slitherlinkshiny — Interactive Slitherlink Puzzle
text: load locally source:

title: slitherlinkshiny — Interactive Slitherlink Puzzle
text: Features: Click near segment cycle state: empty → drawn → crossed → empty Status indicator shows progress, Constraint violated, Puzzle solved! MM:SS timer starts first click freezes puzzle solved

title: slitherlinkshiny — Interactive Slitherlink Puzzle
code: list_puzzles()                  # browse the built-in library g <- get_puzzle("medium_4x4")   # load a puzzle (segments reset to empty) g <- random_puzzle(n = 5, m = 5, seed = 42)  # generate a random puzzle

title: slitherlinkshiny — Interactive Slitherlink Puzzle
code: # Build a grid from a clue matrix (NA = unconstrained cell) clues <- matrix(c(2, 1, 2,                   1, 0, 1,                   2, 1, 2), nrow = 3, byrow = TRUE) g <- init_grid(clues)  # Toggle a segment (0 → 1 → -1 → 0) g <- toggle_segment(g, type = "h", i = 1, j = 1)  # horizontal g <- toggle_segment(g, type = "v", i = 2, j = 3)  # vertical  print(g)  # ASCII display in the console

title: slitherlinkshiny — Interactive Slitherlink Puzzle
code: check_clues(g)               # no clue exceeded (in-progress check) check_clues(g, strict = TRUE)# all clues exactly matched check_loop(g)                # segments form a single closed loop is_solved(g)                 # full solution check

title: slitherlinkshiny — Interactive Slitherlink Puzzle
text: solve_grid() returns NULL solution exists.
code: sol <- solve_grid(g)   # constraint propagation + backtracking is_solved(sol)         # TRUE

title: slitherlinkshiny — Interactive Slitherlink Puzzle
text: slitherlink_grid object contains three matrices: Segment values: 0 = empty, 1 = drawn, -1 = crossed.
